_description: ADO Service Hook configurations for Make.com webhook integration. Create TWO service hooks — one for alert created, one for state changed.

service_hooks.name: GHAzDO Alert Created → Make.com

trigger.description: Fires when a new GHAzDO security alert is detected (secret, code scan, or dependency)

filters._note: Optionally filter to specific repos or alert types if needed

setup_steps: 1. Go to ADO → Project Settings → Service hooks
3. Select 'Web Hooks' from the service list
5. Filters: Leave defaults (all repos, all alert types)
6. Action URL: Paste your Make.com webhook URL

service_hooks.name: GHAzDO Alert State Changed → Make.com

trigger.description: Fires when a GHAzDO alert state changes (active → fixed, dismissed, etc.)

filters._note: Optionally filter to only 'fixed' state if you only want auto-close on resolution

setup_steps: 1. Go to ADO → Project Settings → Service hooks
3. Select 'Web Hooks' from the service list
6. Action URL: Paste the SAME Make.com webhook URL

important_notes: Both service hooks use the SAME Make.com webhook URL — the Router module inside Make.com handles branching by eventType
Test BOTH hooks after creation to confirm Make.com receives the payloads
Service hooks fire immediately on alert events — no polling delay
